rulings.Case Title: LEO MILLER et al VS. AA PLYWOOD, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc'S Motion To Transfer Venue Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure § 397(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 21, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION Joinder In Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc.'s Motion To Transfer Venue Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure § 397(C) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUBMIT DECLARATIONS THAT MEET THE REQUISITE LEVEL OF SPECIFICITY TO GRANT THIS DISCRETIONARY MOTION SEE LIEPPMAN V. LIEBER (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 914 AND JUNEAU V. JUNEAU (1941) 45 CAL.APP.2ND 14. PLAINTIFFS' DECLARATIONS ALSO NEGATE DEFENDANT'S DECLARATIONS. FINALLY, THE MOTION WOULD GREATLY PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO A SPEEDY TRIAL. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: 370 7TH STREET HOMEOWERS ASSOCIATION VS. SJK DEVELOPMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Sjk Development, Inc. And Sean J. Keighran
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 21, 2008, line 2, CROSS-COMPLAINANT SJK DEVELOPMENT, SEAN KEIGHRAN Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Sjk Development, Inc. And Sean J. Keighran. THE SETTLEMENT IS APPROVED. THE DECLARATIONS OF THE MOVING PARTIES PROVIDES AN OBJECTIVE BASIS FOR THE ALLOCATION. THE OPPOSING PARTIES HAVE NOT MADE A FACTUAL SHOWING THAT THE ALLOCATION IS IMPROPER OR COLLUSIVE. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ETHEL GRANT VS. PRIME MORTGAGE FUNDING, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 21, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT BROOKSAMERICA MORTGAGE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION PAPERS, PLAINTIFF SETS FORTH CERTAIN FACTS AND SUGGESTS OTHERS THAT ARE NOT REFLECTED IN THE COMPLAINT. IN ITS PRESENT FORM, THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ESTABLISH A DUTY OWED TO PLAINTIFF BY DEFENDANT UNDER ANY OF PLAINTIFF'S THEORIES. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 21, 2008, line 9, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction IS OFF CALENDAR, CASE REMOVED TO US DISTRICT COURT.
